channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says progress is being made on deal to end Iran war
item.description: President Trump insists Iran is looking to make a deal but questions remain about how the war will end.
item.content:encoded: President Trump insists Iran is looking to make a deal but questions remain about how the war will end.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: Rep. Chris Deluzio, D-Pa, on proposed legislation to cut off funding for Iran war
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Congressman Chris Deluzio of Pennsylvania, one of 18 lawmakers backing new legislation that would cut off funding for the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks with Democratic Congressman Chris Deluzio of Pennsylvania, one of 18 lawmakers backing new legislation that would cut off funding for the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Gas prices keep rising, but do big oil companies plan to drill more? Not so far
item.description: The war in Iran has pushed global oil prices higher, which boosts oil company revenues. But major U.S. oil companies aren't signaling plans to increase production to bring down prices at the pump.
item.content:encoded:
The war in Iran has pushed global oil prices higher, which boosts oil company revenues. But major U.S. oil companies aren't signaling plans to increase production to bring down prices at the pump.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: In a new poll, Americans voice broad bipartisan support for age caps in Congress
item.description: The vast majority of Americans — 8 in 10 — say there should be age caps for members of Congress, as well as term limits, according to the latest NPR/PBS News/Marist poll.
item.content:encoded:
The vast majority of Americans — 8 in 10 — say there should be age caps for members of Congress, as well as term limits, according to the latest NPR/PBS News/Marist poll.

(Image credit: Allison Robbert)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Campaign staffers tell NPR they make 'thousands' betting on their own candidates
item.description: Campaign staffers are turning private polling data into personal paydays. They describe the election prediction market as a "Wild West" for staffers.
item.content:encoded: Campaign staffers are turning private polling data into personal paydays. They describe the election prediction market as a "Wild West" for staffers.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett

item.title: A giant tsunami in Alaska last year was a near-miss in a tourist location, study says
item.description: A new study finds that a megatsunami in Alaska pushed water more than 1,500 feet up the sides of a fjord near Juneau. Researchers say more monitoring is needed to prevent future catastrophes.
item.content:encoded: A new study finds that a megatsunami in Alaska pushed water more than 1,500 feet up the sides of a fjord near Juneau. Researchers say more monitoring is needed to prevent future catastrophes.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Hersher

item.title: Alabama's legislature poised to adopt new congressional map after SCOTUS ruling
item.description: The Alabama Legislature has spent the week debating the state's congressional maps. Democratic lawmakers have argued their voting powers are being diluted because of a Supreme Court ruling.
item.content:encoded: The Alabama Legislature has spent the week debating the state's congressional maps. Democratic lawmakers have argued their voting powers are being diluted because of a Supreme Court ruling.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Elliott

item.title: Affected by the Iran war, residents in Southeast Asian countries voice their concerns
item.description: Southeast Asian countries are voicing concern about President Trump and the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Southeast Asian countries are voicing concern about President Trump and the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michael Sullivan

item.title: The real-world influence of Star Trek's U.S.S. Enterprise
item.description: The Star Trek Enterprise evoked Americans' sense of adventure and a utopian vision of humanity. It's part of NPR's 250th anniversary series "America in Pursuit."
item.content:encoded: The Star Trek Enterprise evoked Americans' sense of adventure and a utopian vision of humanity. It's part of NPR's 250th anniversary series "America in Pursuit."
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after Trump's criticism
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after critical comments by President Trump.
item.content:encoded: Secretary of State Marco Rubio meets with Pope Leo after critical comments by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Megan Williams
